title: 10 best herbal teas for wheezing
intro: Among the top herbal teas for wheezing are licorice root, thyme, and eucalyptus teas. Licorice root tea soothes inflamed throat and airways, promoting easier breathing. Thyme tea, with its antibacterial and antispasmodic properties, relaxes the muscles around the airways and fights respiratory infections. Eucalyptus tea opens up airways and loosens mucus, making it easier to expel. These teas offer effective, natural alternatives for those seeking relief from wheezing.

teas.tea_desc: Peppermint tea aids in alleviating wheezing due to its menthol content, a natural decongestant. This substance works by relaxing the muscles of the respiratory tract, thus reducing inflammation and widening the airways. Consequently, breathing becomes easier, and wheezing decreases. Moreover, peppermint tea's soothing warmth helps to alleviate tension in the chest, further easing discomfort and promoting clearer breathing. Finally, this herbal tea carries antioxidant and antispasmodic properties, both of which contribute to minimizing inflammation and preventing spasms in the airways, thereby significantly reducing wheezing.
tea_parts: Leaves: Rich in menthol, the leaves of the peppermint tea plant help relax the smooth muscles of the respiratory tract, alleviating wheezing.
Flowers: Packed with rosmarinic acid, peppermint flowers possess anti-inflammatory properties that soothe airways, reducing wheezing.
Seeds: A natural source of essential oils, peppermint seeds can help loosen phlegm, making breathing easier for those experiencing wheezing.
tea_constituents: Menthol: A natural compound with cooling and analgesic properties that helps to soothe inflamed airways and relieve wheezing.
Rosmarinic acid: A potent anti-inflammatory compound that inhibits the release of histamines and prostaglandins, reducing inflammation in the airways and alleviating wheezing.
Limonene: A monoterpene with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that helps to reduce airway inflammation and ease breathing.
Methyl salicylate: A methyl ester with analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that helps to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the airways, providing relief from wheezing.
tea_recipe: Gather 1 cup of fresh peppermint leaves or 1 tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves.
Boil 2 cups of water and add the peppermint leaves.
Let the mixture steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the leaves.
Drink the tea while still warm, up to three times a day for wheezing relief.

teas.tea_desc: Licorice tea aids in alleviating wheezing due to its active compounds, such as glycyrrhizin, which possess anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds work by reducing swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Moreover, licorice tea encourages expectoration, helping to clear mucus from the respiratory tract and further easing wheezing. This soothing action on the throat and airways also helps to minimize irritation, providing quick relief. Finally, licorice tea's demulcent qualities coat and protect the mucous membranes, diminishing the intensity of coughing and wheezing fits.
tea_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice tea plant contain glycyrrhizin, which helps reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the licorice tea plant have expectorant properties, aiding in the expulsion of mucus and alleviating wheezing.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice tea plant are a rich source of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce wheezing.
Flowers: The flowers of the licorice tea plant contain essential oils that have a soothing effect on the respiratory system, providing relief from wheezing.
Seeds: The seeds of the licorice tea plant contain active compounds that help relax the bronchial smooth muscles, reducing wheezing.
tea_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Possesses anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce airway inflammation, alleviating wheezing.
Liquiritigenin: Acts as a antispasmodic, helping to relax airway muscles and reducing wheezing.
Glabridin: Exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, protecting lung tissue and reducing wheezing.
Liquiritin: Helps soothe irritated respiratory tracts, providing relief from wheezing.
isoliquiritigenin: Inhibits the release of histamine, reducing allergic reactions and associated wheezing.
tea_recipe: Measure - Pour 8 ounces of water into a saucepan and add 1 tablespoon of dried licorice root.
Heat - Place the saucepan on a stove and bring the water to a boil.
Steep - Reduce heat to low, cover the saucepan, and let the licorice root simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain - Pour the tea through a fine-mesh strainer into a cup, discarding the licorice root.
Serve - Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day to alleviate wheezing.

teas.tea_desc: Chamomile tea contains anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties due to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids. These compounds help reduce inflammation in the airways, nasal passages, and lungs, thus alleviating wheezing. Furthermore, chamomile tea promotes relaxation and stress reduction, which can diminish symptoms of asthma and wheezing caused by anxiety or tension. Additionally, its mucolytic effect thins and loosens mucus, making it easier to expel, and its antihistamine properties help counteract allergic reactions that can cause wheezing. Ultimately, the combination of these properties in chamomile tea brings about a soothing effect on the respiratory system, providing relief from wheezing.
tea_parts: Flowers: The chamomile tea commonly consumed for wheezing is primarily made from the daisy-like flowers of the chamomile plant. These flowers contain numerous terpenoids and flavonoids with anti-inflammatory and relaxant properties, helping to alleviate bronchial spasms and reduce inflammation in the airways.

Leaves: The chamomile leaves, although less commonly used than the flowers, can also be utilized when making medicinal tea for wheezing. These leaves contain volatile oils that can help relax the smooth muscles of the respiratory tract, thus easing the breathing difficulties associated with wheezing.

Seeds: While sometimes overlooked, the chamomile seeds can be employed when preparing medicinal tea for wheezing. The seeds are a rich source of antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help in reducing inflammation of the respiratory tract, thereby providing relief from wheezing symptoms.
tea_constituents: Alpha-pinene: A compound with anti-inflammatory properties that helps reduce swelling in the airways, making breathing easier.
Bisabolol: A terpene with antispasmodic effects that can help relax muscles in the respiratory system, alleviating wheezing.
Chamazulene: A compound that has been shown to inhibit the production of leukotrienes, chemicals that cause airway inflammation and constriction.
Matricin: A precursor to chamazulene that also possesses anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce wheezing by minimizing airway inflammation.
Apigenin: A flavonoid that acts as a natural bronchodilator, expanding the airways and improving airflow to the lungs.
tea_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water to 212°F (100°C) for 1-3 minutes.
Add 2 teaspoons of dried chamomile flowers to a tea infuser.
Pour the boiling water over the chamomile flowers and steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser to prevent over-steeping.
Sip the warm chamomile tea slowly to alleviate wheezing.

teas.tea_desc: Slippery elm tea provides relief from wheezing due to its mucilage content, which transforms into a gel-like substance when combined with water. This gel coats the mouth, throat, and lungs, reducing inflammation and irritation. As a result, airways open up, making it easier to breathe. By forming a protective barrier, slippery elm tea minimizes the need to cough or wheeze, thus soothing respiratory issues. Furthermore, its anti-inflammatory and demulcent properties help alleviate the symptoms of asthma and other respiratory conditions characterized by wheezing.
tea_parts: Bark: The bark of the slippery elm tree is used to make medicinal tea for wheezing due to its high mucilage content, which helps soothe irritated throats and reduce coughing.
Leaves: Slippery elm leaves are used in teas for wheezing because they contain antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate respiratory issues.
Seeds: The seeds of the slippery elm plant are used in teas to help relieve wheezing, as they contain a slimy substance that coats and soothes the mucous membranes lining the respiratory tract.
Roots: The roots of the slippery elm are used in teas to help reduce wheezing, as they are rich in mucilage, which helps to lubricate and soothe irritated airways.
tea_constituents: Tannins: Tannins possess astringent properties that help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating wheezing.
Mucilage: The mucilage in slippery elm tea forms a soothing film over the mucous membranes, easing irritation and reducing wheezing.
Gallic acid: Gallic acid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to decrease inflammation and ease breathing in cases of wheezing.
Kaempferol: Kaempferol is a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate airway inflammation and reduce wheezing.
Eugenol: Eugenol hasexpectorant and soothing properties, helping to loosen phlegm and relieve wheezing caused by chest congestion.
tea_recipe: Obtain 1-2 teaspoons of dried slippery elm bark.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the bark in a cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove bark particles.
Drink the tea up to 3 times a day for wheezing relief.

teas.tea_desc: Ginger tea aids in alleviating wheezing due to its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling in the airways. Its potent antioxidants help maintain healthy lung function by combating free radicals. Furthermore, ginger tea's natural expectorant qualities loosen phlegm, making it easier to expel mucus and clear airways. Additionally, ginger tea's soothing warmth relaxes constricted airways, providing instant relief from wheezing. Lastly, ginger tea's ability to inhibit airway contraction prevents the onset of wheezing episodes.
tea_parts: Rhizomes: These are underground stems that are used in making medicinal tea for wheezing due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways.
Leaves: The leaves of the ginger tea plant are used in making tea to alleviate wheezing as they contain compounds that have bronchodilatory effects, helping to widen the airways.

Flowers: The flowers of the ginger tea plant are used in making medicinal tea to treat wheezing due to their antioxidant and expectorant properties, which can help clear mucus and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: The seeds of the ginger tea plant are used in making tea to alleviate wheezing because they contain compounds that can help relax the muscles in the airways, reducing spasms and inflammation.

Roots: The roots of the ginger tea plant are used in making medicinal tea for wheezing due to their expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties, which can help clear mucus, reduce inflammation, and alleviate pain.
tea_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols are compounds in ginger that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate wheezing.
Shogaols: Shogaols are compounds in ginger that have bronchodilatory effects, helping to widen the airways and improve breathing in people with wheezing.
Zingerone: Zingerone is a compound in ginger that has antioxidant properties, helping to protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to wheezing.
Paradols: Paradols are compounds in ginger that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, helping to relax the smooth muscles of the airways and reduce wheezing.
Gingerdiols: Gingerdiols are compounds in ginger that have expectorant properties, helping to remove mucus from the respiratory tract and alleviate wheezing.
tea_recipe: Peel and grate 1 inch of fresh ginger, then let it steep in boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the ginger pieces from the water, leaving behind the ginger-infused tea.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey and 1/2 lemon juice to the ginger tea, stirring until the honey dissolves.
Warm up the mixture on low heat for 2 minutes, ensuring it doesn't reach a boil.
Pour the ginger tea into a cup and sip it slowly to alleviate wheezing.

teas.tea_desc: Marshmallow tea, derived from the marshmallow plant's root, contains mucilage, a substance known for its soothing properties. This mucilage creates a protective layer over the throat and respiratory tract, reducing irritation. As a result, the frequency and severity of wheezing episodes decrease. Additionally, this natural remedy has anti-inflammatory effects, further alleviating respiratory distress. Marshmallow tea's expectorant qualities aid in the elimination of excess mucus, promoting clearer airways and easier breathing. Ultimately, regularly consuming marshmallow tea brings consistent relief to those suffering from wheezing.
tea_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory properties, leaves help reduce airway inflammation, alleviating wheezing.
Flowers: Contain calming compounds, flowers help soothe nervous system, reducing wheezing caused by anxiety or excitement.
Stems: Packed with antispasmodic properties, stems aid in relaxing muscles around airways, easing wheezing.
Roots: High in expectorant properties, roots help clear mucus from airways, providing relief from wheezing.
Bark: Abundant in antioxidants, bark aids in protecting lungs from damage, reducing wheezing.
tea_constituents: Mucilage: This constituent forms a viscous layer on the mucous membranes, providing a soothing effect and reducing irritation that can cause wheezing.
Flavonoids: These antioxidants have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, relieving wheezing.
Phenolic acids: These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties, which can help alleviate wheezing and respiratory issues.
Ascorbic acid: This vitamin acts as a potent antioxidant and supports the immune system, which is crucial for managing respiratory conditions linked to wheezing.
tea_recipe: Measure 1 tablespoon of dried marshmallow root and place it in a tea infuser.
Boil 2 cups of water and pour it over the marshmallow root in the infuser.
Steep the tea for 10 minutes to allow the active compounds to release.
Remove the infuser and let the tea cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 1 cup of marshmallow tea, up to three times a day, to soothe a wheezing cough.

teas.tea_desc: Fenugreek tea aids in alleviating wheezing due to its expectorant properties, which help to thin and expel mucus from the respiratory tract. This herbal tea contains compounds like mucilage, which soothes irritated membranes in the nose and throat, reducing inflammation and easing breathing. Furthermore, fenugreek tea's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects target the underlying causes of wheezing, providing relief for asthma and bronchitis symptoms. Additionally, fenugreek's high levels of steroidal saponins contribute to its bronchodilatory action, promoting smooth muscle relaxation and increasing airflow to the lungs. Finally, the tea's antioxidant properties support overall respiratory health, strengthening the immune system and protecting against oxidative stress.
tea_parts: Seeds: Fenugreek seeds are rich in mucilage, a type of fiber that turns into a gel when mixed with water. This gel coats the respiratory tract, providing relief from wheezing.
Leaves: Fenugreek leaves contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate wheezing.
Flowers: Fenugreek flowers are a source of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, that have been shown to have bronchodilatory effects, helping to widen the airways and reduce wheezing.
Rhyzomes: Fenugreek rhyzomes, like the seeds, are rich in mucilage. This means they can help to soothe irritated airways and reduce wheezing.
Roots: Fenugreek roots contain coumarins, which have been shown to have antispasmodic properties. This means they can help to relax the muscles in the respiratory tract and reduce wheezing.
tea_constituents: Fenugreek Seed Extract: This constituent contains elements that help relax muscles in the airways and reduce inflammation, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing.
Diosgenin: This compound found in fenugreek has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating wheezing symptoms.
Trigonelline: This alkaloid found in fenugreek can help improve lung function and reduce inflammation, reducing symptoms of wheezing.
Gallic Acid: This polyphenol compound has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating wheezing symptoms.
Quercetin: This flavonoid found in fenugreek has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation in the airways, reducing wheezing.
Choline: This essential nutrient in fenugreek can help relax muscles in the airways and reduce inflammation, alleviating symptoms of wheezing.
tea_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of dried fenugreek seeds and let steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea, discarding the seeds.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to soothe wheezing.

teas.tea_desc: Turmeric tea reduces wheezing by providing an anti-inflammatory effect, thanks to its active ingredient, curcumin. This compound restricts the activation of molecules that trigger inflammation in airways. Furthermore, curcumin loosens mucus, facilitating its elimination and reducing airway blockage, consequently alleviating wheezing. Additionally, turmeric tea's antioxidant properties counterbalance free radicals, thus preventing lung tissue damage. Finally, it enhances lung function by relaxing muscles around the airways, further minimizing wheezing.
tea_parts: Rhizomes: These underground stems of turmeric tea plant contain curcumin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate wheezing by reducing inflammation in the airways.
Leaves: High in antioxidants, turmeric leaves can help reduce oxidative stress and improve lung function, providing relief from wheezing.
Flowers: Rich in essential oils and antioxidants, turmeric flowers can help soothe respiratory inflammation and provide relief from wheezing.
Seeds: Turmeric seeds contain a high concentration of curcumin, making them a potent anti-inflammatory agent that can help alleviate wheezing.
Buds: Turmeric buds are rich in essential oils and antioxidants, making them effective in reducing inflammation and providing relief from wheezing.
tea_constituents: Curcumin: A potent anti-inflammatory agent that reduces airway inflammation and alleviates wheezing.
Turmerone: A compound with bronchodilator properties that widens the airways, improving breathing and easing wheezing.
Curdione: An antioxidant that protects lung tissues from damage, supporting respiratory health and reducing wheezing.
Bisacurone: A compound with antispasmodic effects that can help relieve bronchial spasms and subsequent wheezing.
Germacrone: A compound with anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, aiding in the clearance of mucus and reducing wheezing.
tea_recipe: Boil 4 cups of water in a pot for 5 minutes.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of grated turmeric root and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, then stir in 1 teaspoon of honey and 1/4 teaspoon of cinnamon.
Add 1/2 cup of almond milk and warm the tea on low heat for 2 minutes.
Pour the tea into a cup and enjoy for immediate wheezing relief.

teas.tea_desc: Elderberry tea helps with wheezing because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the respiratory passages, allowing for easier breathing. Its rich supply of antioxidants strengthens the immune system, helping to fight off respiratory infections that can lead to wheezing. Elderberry tea also has expectorant properties that help loosen and expel mucus from the airways, reducing congestion and making breathing easier. Additionally, elderberry tea soothes irritated throats, alleviating coughing and decreasing wheezing. Finally, its natural diuretic properties help remove toxins from the body, cleansing the respiratory system and further reducing wheezing.
tea_constituents: Anthocyanins: These are water-soluble pigments that give elderberries their distinctive dark purple color. They have strong anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating wheezing.

Flavonoids: These are a type of antioxidant that can help protect cells from damage. They have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help reduce inflammation and fight off respiratory infections that can cause wheezing.

Chlorogenic acid: This is a type of polyphenol that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties. It can help reduce inflammation in the airways and fight off respiratory infections that can cause wheezing.

Rutin: This is a type of flavonoid that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It can help reduce inflammation in the airways and strengthen capillaries, improving respiratory health and alleviating wheezing.

Caffeic acid: This is a type of phenolic acid that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties. It can help reduce inflammation in the airways and fight off respiratory infections that can cause wheezing.
tea_recipe: In a saucepan, combine 1 cup of elderberries with 2 cups of water, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove elderberries, then add lemon juice and honey to taste.
Pour the tea into a mug and let it cool to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 1 cup of the tea up to 3 times a day to alleviate wheezing.
tea_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and tannins, elderberry flowers promote respiratory health and alleviate wheezing.
Leaves: Containing rosmarinic acid and caffeic malic acid, elderberry leaves help reduce inflammation and soothe respiratory symptoms.
Berries: Packed with vitamins and antioxidants, elderberry berries strengthen the immune system and provide relief from wheezing.
Bark: Source of lectins and phenolic acids, elderberry bark aids in reducing inflammation and easing breathing difficulties.

teas.tea_desc: Nettle tea combats wheezing by reducing inflammation in the airways, thanks to its anti-inflammatory properties. This herbal tea contains rosmarinic acid, a compound known to alleviate symptoms of asthma, including wheezing. Furthermore, nettle tea boasts bronchodilatory effects, facilitating the opening of the airways and thus easing brearhability. Additionally, it provides rich stores of antioxidants, which aid in safeguarding the lungs from oxidative stress and damage. Finally, nettle tea's expectorant qualities help clear mucus from the respiratory tract, further alleviating wheezing.
tea_parts: Leaves: Nettle tea made from leaves is often used for respiratory conditions like wheezing due to their richness in anti-inflammatory compounds.
Stems: The stems of nettle tea plant are used in making medicinal tea for wheezing because of their expectorant properties that help clear airways.
Flowers: Nettle tea flowers contain soothing mucilage, making them an ideal addition to a tea aimed at alleviating wheezing.
Seeds: Nettle seeds are used in medicinal tea for wheezing due to their high levels of nutrients and antioxidants that support respiratory health.
tea_constituents: Histamine: Acts as a bronchodilator, helping to open up airways and reduce wheezing.
Acetylcholine: Promotes muscle relaxation, helping to alleviate constriction of airways and lessen wheezing.
Chlorogenic acid: Possesses anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate wheezing.
Kaempferol: Acts as an antioxidant, helping to protect the lungs from damage and reduce wheezing.
Quercetin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and protect the lungs from damage, thereby alleviating wheezing.
tea_recipe: Gather 2-3 handfuls of fresh nettle leaves, rinse them thoroughly, and let them soak in water for 10 minutes to remove any dirt or impurities.
Boil 4 cups of water in a pot, then add the rinsed nettle leaves and let them simmer for 5 minutes to release their natural oils and therapeutic compounds.
Remove the pot from heat, cover it, and let the tea steep for 10-15 minutes to enhance its flavor and health benefits.
Strain the tea into a cup or mug, discarding the spent nettle leaves, and let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Consume 1-2 cups of nettle tea up to 3 times a day to relieve wheezing and soothe respiratory symptoms.

other_remedies: Common and effective natural remedies for wheezing include deep breathing exercises, such as pursed-lips breathing and diaphragmatic breathing. Pursed-lips breathing involves inhaling through the nose and exhaling through pursed lips as if blowing out a candle. This technique helps slow down breathing and increases the amount of air that stays in the lungs. Diaphragmatic breathing, on the other hand, involves using the diaphragm, the main muscle of respiration, to breathe in and out. This can be done by lying on one's back and placing a book on the abdomen while focusing on breathing in a way that moves the book up and down. Additionally, staying hydrated by drinking plenty of fluids, especially water, can help thin mucus and reduce wheezing. Regular physical activity and maintaining a healthy weight can also help reduce the frequency and severity of wheezing. It is important to consult with a healthcare provider to determine the underlying cause of wheezing and to develop an appropriate treatment plan.
causes: Asthma: Asthma is a chronic condition that causes inflammation and narrowing of the airways, resulting in wheezing.
Respiratory infections: Infections such as bronchitis, pneumonia, or the flu can cause inflammation and mucus production in the airways, leading to wheezing.
Allergies: Allergic reactions to substances such as pollen, dust mites, or pet dander can cause inflammation and swelling of the airways, resulting in wheezing.
Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease: COPD is a group of lung diseases, including chronic bronchitis and emphysema, that cause narrowing of the airways and mucus production, resulting in wheezing.
Anaphylaxis: A severe allergic reaction can cause airway constriction and swelling, leading to wheezing.
Medications: Certain medications, such as beta-blockers, can cause the airways to narrow, resulting in wheezing.
Gastroesophageal reflux disease: GERD can cause stomach acid to back up into the throat, irritating the airways and resulting in wheezing.
Vocal cord dysfunction: Vocal cord dysfunction is a condition in which the vocal cords do not open properly during breathing, causing wheezing.
Foreign object inhalation: Breathing in a foreign object, such as a toy or food, can cause airway obstruction and wheezing.
definition: Wheezing is a high-pitched whistling or rasping sound that occurs during breathing, typically during exhale. This symptom results from narrowed or constricted airways in the lungs, often due to conditions such as asthma, bronchitis, or emphysema. Wheezing impacts people's lives by causing breathing difficulties, sleep disturbances, fatigue, and limiting daily activities. Severe wheezing can lead to oxygen deprivation, putting strain on the heart and other organs, ultimately reducing one's overall quality of life and potentially leading to life-threatening situations if left untreated.
